midnight-canvas.$description: Midnight Canvas — Page background, hero background, primary canvas — deep near-black navy that absorbs the eye and makes artwork pop

abyss.$description: Abyss — Deepest surface layer, navigation background — the near-pure black undercarriage beneath the canvas

eclipse.$description: Eclipse — Footer background, alternate dark surface — slightly warmer dark for depth variation

carbon.$description: Carbon — Elevated dark surface — used for cards or panels sitting above the canvas

slate-veil.$description: Slate Veil — Neutral form states, badge text, and quiet UI feedback where color should stay understated. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

luminous.$description: Luminous — Primary text, heading text, icon fills, link text, button labels — the dominant light element across the interface

pearl.$description: Pearl — Border color used at scale (hairs, dividers, card edges) — the structural skeleton of the UI

mist.$description: Mist — Body text, secondary text, helper copy — muted gray for non-primary reading

fog.$description: Fog — List text, icon fills, secondary metadata — the lightest level of secondary text

deep-ink.$description: Deep Ink — Gray text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

onyx-label.$description: Onyx Label — Dark text for inverted or high-contrast button labels on light fills

electric-cyan.$description: Electric Cyan — Primary CTA buttons (SIGN UP NOW, plan tier cards) — the sole chromatic action color; neon-cyan against the void creates cinema-marquee urgency

teal-pulse.$description: Teal Pulse — Accent text, promotional price highlights (€4.99), link emphasis, body emphasis — the slightly darker teal sibling used for inline chromatic moments that aren't buttons

inspire.$description: Sole typeface across the entire interface — headings, body, buttons, labels, nav. The single-font commitment is deliberate: it means Inspire must carry both 12px micro-labels and 40px display moments without losing character. Use 700 for headings, price, and emphasis; 400 for everything else.

abyss.$description: Surface level 0: Deepest background, navigation chrome

midnight-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Primary page and hero background

eclipse.$description: Surface level 2: Footer and alternate dark bands

carbon.$description: Surface level 3: Elevated cards and panels above the canvas

slate-veil.$description: Surface level 4: Input fields and border-defined surfaces
